articles.title: OpenAI’s former Sora boss is leaving
articles.summary: OpenAI's Sora team leader Bill Peebles is departing after the company discontinued its video generation tool. OpenAI is refocusing on coding and enterprise products, phasing out side projects.

articles.title: Should you stare into Sam Altman’s orb before your next date?
articles.summary: Tinder now offers five free boosts to users who verify their identity through World's iris-scanning orbs, co-founded by Sam Altman. The service is expanding from Japan to select markets including the United States.

articles.title: Anthropic’s new cybersecurity model could get it back in the government’s good graces
articles.summary: Anthropic's new cybersecurity model Claude Mythos Preview may improve relations with the Trump administration after weeks of tension. The administration previously criticized the company but appears interested in its security-focused AI capabilities.

articles.title: The App Store is booming again, and AI may be why
articles.summary: App Store experiences significant growth in 2026 with increased app launches. AI tools are credited as a major driver behind the mobile software boom.

articles.title: Sam Altman’s project World looks to scale its human verification empire. First stop: Tinder.
articles.summary: World, Sam Altman's human verification project using Orb technology, expands through new partnerships including Tinder to scale its anonymous verification services.

articles.title: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles exit OpenAI as company continues to shed ‘side quests’
articles.summary: OpenAI executives Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles departed as the company discontinues Sora and its science division, refocusing on enterprise AI over consumer projects.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million Series B to build AI-native infrastructure competing against AWS and Google Cloud.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges up to $200/month for AI coding assistance, but Goose offers identical functionality for free as an open-source alternative that runs locally without subscription fees or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge that attracted thousands of applicants and helped recruit talented engineers.

articles.title: US-sanctioned currency exchange says $15 million heist done by "unfriendly states"
articles.summary: Kyrgyzstan-registered cryptocurrency exchange Grinex halted operations after a $15 million heist. The exchange blamed state-sponsored hackers from 'unfriendly states' for the attack targeting Russian users.

articles.title: Recent advances push Big Tech closer to the Q-Day danger zone
articles.summary: Recent advances in quantum computing bring the cryptographic 'Q-Day' closer, where current encryption becomes vulnerable. The Flame malware's MD5 exploit demonstrates how broken cryptography poses catastrophic security risks globally.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Nutanix claims to have attracted thousands of VMware customers due to dissatisfaction with Broadcom's VMware takeover. The rival company now has approximately 30,000 customers, many migrating from VMware.

articles.title: Pie Day 2026
articles.summary: Ellie organized a Pi Day 2026 event at MIT's Food Institute, coordinating the baking of 30 pies. The posts detail her Pi Day celebration and the behind-the-scenes process of pie preparation.

articles.title: The Download: bad news for inner Neanderthals, and AI warfare’s human illusion
articles.summary: French geneticists challenge the theory that humans carry Neanderthal DNA from ancient interbreeding, proposing population structure offers an alternative explanation for genetic similarities.

articles.title: The case for fixing everything
articles.summary: Stewart Brand's new book 'Maintenance: Of Everything, Part One' argues that repair and upkeep of tools and systems are civilizationally important and can be radical acts deserving honor.

articles.title: Building a Fast Multilingual OCR Model with Synthetic Data
articles.summary: Researchers developed a fast multilingual OCR model using synthetic data, improving text recognition speed and accuracy across multiple languages without requiring extensive real-world training datasets.

articles.title: Ecom-RLVE: Adaptive Verifiable Environments for E-Commerce Conversational Agents
articles.summary: Ecom-RLVE introduces adaptive verifiable environments for training e-commerce conversational agents. The system enables agents to learn through reinforcement learning in realistic shopping scenarios with verification capabilities.

articles.title: The PR you would have opened yourself
articles.summary: An article about pull requests that developers would have created themselves, likely discussing code contributions and development practices.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of artificial intelligence, exploring emerging trends and developments in the AI field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing initiatives.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has released an open-source toolkit designed to advance agricultural practices through technology. The 'farm of the future' initiative aims to help farmers leverage AI and digital tools.

articles.title: 7 ways to travel smarter this summer, with help from Google
articles.summary: Google's latest tools help travelers plan trips, find deals, and explore destinations more efficiently this summer.

articles.title: A new way to explore the web with AI Mode in Chrome
articles.summary: Chrome's AI Mode receives upgrades that transform web interaction, enabling users to explore and engage with online content more effectively through artificial intelligence capabilities.

articles.title: New ways to create personalized images in the Gemini app
articles.summary: Nano Banana 2 now generates personalized images using your personal context and Google Photos library, creating visuals that reflect your unique life and experiences.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS model offers granular audio tags for precise control over expressive AI-generated speech, enabling more nuanced audio generation.

articles.title: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: Powering real-world robotics tasks through enhanced embodied reasoning
articles.summary: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6 enhances autonomous robots with improved spatial reasoning and multi-view understanding capabilities for real-world task execution.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google released Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence byte for byte.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and higher per-token pricing. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launched DLSS 5, a generative AI-powered graphics technology that enhances photorealism in real-time up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate detailed visuals.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 covers major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, and ChatGPT's interactive math visuals. Discussions include Nemotron 3 Super, xAI updates, and an Anthropic lawsuit.

articles.title: OpenAI Agents SDK improves governance with sandbox execution
articles.summary: OpenAI introduces sandbox execution in its Agents SDK, enabling enterprises to deploy automated workflows with controlled risk and improved governance while balancing flexibility with security.

articles.title: Cadence expands AI and robotic partnerships with Nvidia, Google Cloud
articles.summary: Cadence Design Systems expanded partnerships with Nvidia and Google Cloud to combine AI with physics-based simulation for robotic systems and semiconductor design. The collaboration integrates Cadence's simulation tools with Nvidia's CUDA-X and Omniverse for real-world system modeling.

articles.title: Commvault launches a ‘Ctrl-Z’ for cloud AI workloads
articles.summary: Commvault launched AI Protect to monitor and rollback autonomous AI agents' actions across cloud platforms. The system addresses governance challenges as AI agents exhibit unpredictable behavior unlike traditional static permission models.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed automated failure attribution methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems, addressing a key challenge in collaborative AI problem-solving.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to update their own weights through self-editing and reinforcement learning, advancing self-improving AI systems.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distillation optimizes LLMs for speed but reduces their ability to explore alternative solutions on difficult problems, revealing a tradeoff between faster reasoning and weaker generalization.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering harnesses around them emerge as the true competitive advantage. Anthropic's Claude Code leak demonstrates that surrounding infrastructure, not just the core model, defines market moats.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Google AI Releases Auto-Diagnose: An Large Language Model LLM-Based System to Diagnose Integration Test Failures at Scale
articles.summary: Google released Auto-Diagnose, an LLM-based tool that automatically diagnoses integration test failures by analyzing logs and identifying root causes with 90.14% accuracy across thousands of tests.

articles.title: A End-to-End Coding Guide to Running OpenAI GPT-OSS Open-Weight Models with Advanced Inference Workflows
articles.summary: Tutorial on running OpenAI's open-weight GPT-OSS models in Google Colab, covering setup, quantization, and advanced inference workflows including structured generation, streaming, and batch processing.

articles.title: Top 19 AI Red Teaming Tools (2026): Secure Your ML Models
articles.summary: AI red teaming systematically tests ML models against adversarial attacks and security vulnerabilities, going beyond traditional penetration testing to identify AI-specific risks and emergent behaviors.

articles.title: The best TV antenna of 2026: Expert tested
articles.summary: TV antennas provide free access to local channels for news, sports, and entertainment, allowing consumers to eliminate expensive cable subscriptions. Expert-tested antennas are available in indoor and outdoor options.

articles.title: Your old iPad or Android tablet can be your new smart home panel - here's how
articles.summary: Old tablets can be repurposed as smart home control panels. Convert your unused iPad or Android device into a dedicated interface for managing smart home devices.

articles.title: Apple's original AirTag still tracks effectively, and you can get a 4-pack for its best price ever
articles.summary: Apple's original AirTag Gen 1 remains an effective Bluetooth tracking device at nearly half the price of the newer Gen 2 model, with 4-packs available at their lowest price ever.

articles.title: The Complete Guide to Inference Caching in LLMs
articles.summary: Inference caching in LLMs reduces costs and latency when calling large language model APIs at scale by storing and reusing previous computations.

articles.title: Python Decorators for Production Machine Learning Engineering
articles.summary: Article explores using Python decorators for production machine learning engineering, helping developers write cleaner and more maintainable ML code through decorator patterns.

articles.title: 5 Techniques for Efficient Long-Context RAG
articles.summary: Article discusses five techniques for optimizing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems when handling long contextual information, improving efficiency and performance in AI applications.

articles.title: AI Agents Need Their Own Desk, and Git Worktrees Give Them One
articles.summary: Git worktrees enable AI agents to work in parallel coding sessions independently, reducing setup overhead and providing isolated workspaces for agentic development.

articles.title: How to Learn Python for Data Science Fast in 2026 (Without Wasting Time)
articles.summary: Learn efficient strategies for mastering Python for data science in 2026 by avoiding common mistakes made during the learning journey.

articles.title: Beyond Prompting: Using Agent Skills in Data Science
articles.summary: Developer converts eight-year visualization habit into reusable AI agent workflow, moving beyond simple prompting to create structured data science skills.

articles.title: No Need for Space Gear — Capcom’s ‘PRAGMATA’ Joins GeForce NOW on Launch Day
articles.summary: Capcom's sci-fi action game PRAGMATA launches on GeForce NOW the same day as its worldwide release, allowing instant cloud streaming. Five additional titles join the platform this week.

articles.title: Rethinking AI TCO: Why Cost per Token Is the Only Metric That Matters
articles.summary: Data centers have evolved into AI token factories, making cost per token the primary metric for evaluating infrastructure economics. Traditional TCO measurements focusing on chip specs and FLOPS per dollar are becoming obsolete.

articles.title: New Adobe Premiere Color Grading Mode Accelerated on NVIDIA GPUs
articles.summary: Adobe unveils a new Premiere Color Mode in beta at NAB Show 2026, accelerated by NVIDIA RTX GPUs to enhance video editing workflows for professional creators.

articles.title: Introducing granular cost attribution for Amazon Bedrock
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock now offers granular cost attribution for AI inference, automatically tracking costs by IAM principal and supporting cost allocation tags for better financial planning and optimization.

articles.title: Optimize video semantic search intent with Amazon Nova Model Distillation on Amazon Bedrock
articles.summary: Amazon Nova Model Distillation on Bedrock optimizes video semantic search by balancing accuracy and latency. The approach uses intelligent intent routing to reduce end-to-end search time while maintaining strong performance.

articles.title: Power video semantic search with Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings
articles.summary: Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings enable video semantic search, allowing organizations to quickly find specific moments within videos for sports highlights, archived content, and news footage retrieval.

articles.title: Full-Stack Optimizations for Agentic Inference with NVIDIA Dynamo
articles.summary: NVIDIA Dynamo enables full-stack optimizations for agentic inference, allowing AI coding agents to generate production code at scale. Companies like Stripe and Ramp report thousands of agent-generated PRs weekly.

articles.title: Build a More Secure, Always-On Local AI Agent with OpenClaw and NVIDIA NemoClaw
articles.summary: OpenClaw and NVIDIA NemoClaw enable secure, always-on local AI agents that autonomously handle multi-step workflows, file reading, and API calls beyond simple Q&A systems.

articles.title: Accelerate Clean, Modular, Nuclear Reactor Design with AI Physics
articles.summary: AI is accelerating the design of small modular nuclear reactors (SMRs) and Generation IV designs to meet safety, efficiency, and sustainability requirements while improving project economics.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: Research study explores how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms, examining generational attitudes toward advertising.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing and sharing carbon mitigation technology openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background applied unique perspectives to develop innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive breakthroughs in emerging fields.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers present methods for understanding LLM behavior at scale through feature attribution and interaction analysis, advancing AI interpretability and trustworthiness.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses an encoder to map objects to images, with noise corruption in measurements. An information estimator quantifies measurement quality using only noisy data and noise models.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm using divide-and-conquer approach instead of temporal difference learning, offering better scalability for long-horizon tasks.
